trade.summary: Trade is moderate. Indonesia exports palm oil, rubber, paper products, and textiles. Svezia exports machinery, telecommunications equipment (Ericsson), vehicles, and chemicals. Ericsson's telecommunications infrastructure in Indonesia is a significant trade element.

diplomatic.summary: Svezia and Indonesia maintain cordial relations. Svezia has been a provider of development assistance to Indonesia. The EU-ASEAN framework structures much of the diplomatic interaction. Svezia has raised human rights concerns about Indonesia (Papua) but without major disruption to bilateral ties.

military.summary: Limited military relations. Saab has explored opportunities in the Indonesian defence market. No formal military cooperation agreements.

financial.summary: Swedish FDI in Indonesia includes Ericsson, Volvo, H&M supply chain, and IKEA operations. IKEA sources significantly from Indonesian furniture manufacturers. Swedish development aid through Sida has historically supported Indonesian governance and environment programmes.

energy.summary: Limited energy trade. Svezia imports no significant Indonesian energy. Some cooperation on renewable energy and geothermal development.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation mainly in environmental science, forestry, and climate change. Swedish research institutions have partnered with Indonesian counterparts through Sida-funded research programmes. KTH and other Swedish universities have exchange agreements with Indonesian institutions.
